turns.text: Good morning! Thanks for taking the time to meet with us. Could you start by telling me a little about yourself?

Of course. I'm a frontend developer with about three years of experience. I've been working mostly with React and TypeScript, building user-facing features for web applications. I'm really passionate about creating clean, accessible interfaces.
Great background. What made you decide to apply for this position specifically?

I've been following your company for a while and I really admire the product you're building. I feel like my skills in React and design systems would be a strong match, and I'm excited about the opportunity to work on something with real impact.
Can you describe one of your biggest professional strengths?

I'd say my strongest skill is breaking down complex problems into smaller, manageable pieces. When I face something unclear, I tend to ask the right questions first and then work through it step by step.
What about a weakness? Is there something you're actively working on improving?

Honestly, I used to struggle with saying no when I had too much on my plate. I've been working on setting clearer boundaries and communicating early when deadlines are at risk. It's made a big difference in my productivity.
Where do you see yourself in two or three years?

I'd love to grow into a senior developer role and eventually take ownership of a product area. I'm also interested in mentoring junior developers — I think teaching is one of the best ways to solidify your own knowledge.
That sounds like a solid plan. Do you have any questions for us?
Yes, actually. I'd love to understand how the engineering team collaborates with design. Is there a process in place, or is it still evolving?
